heading: Configure Authorization
body:
**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

> **Note:** You can use the same Atlas CLI command to configure LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol)
> authentication and LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization.

To save one LDAP configuration for the project you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas security ldap save [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas security ldap save](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-security-ldap-save).

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

Use the following procedure to configure user authorization with
LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) for all clusters in a project.

> **Important:** 
>
> - You must enable authentication with LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) before enabling
>   authorization.
>
> - When you enable and configure LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization, database users
>   who are only configured for LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authentication will no longer
>   be able to access databases.

When a user attempts to perform an action, Atlas executes the
[LDAP query template](manual/reference/configuration-options)
to obtain the LDAP groups to which the authenticated user belongs.
Atlas permits the action if the query returns at least one group
that is authorized to perform the action. Atlas does not permit
the action if the query returns no groups that are authorized to
perform the action.

Atlas substitutes the authenticated username in the `{USER}`
placeholder when it runs the query. The query is relative to the host
specified in Server Hostname.

The formatting for the query must conform to [RFC4515](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc4515).

If you do not provide a query template, Atlas applies the default
value: `{USER}?memberOf?base`.

Wait for Atlas to deploy your changes. Atlas verifies that
your clusters can connect to, authenticate with, and query your LDAP
server using the configuration details that you provide.
